prompt: What's the current take on isometric training for maintaining strength on squat, bench, and deadlift when equipment is limited?
assertions: States isometrics can work for strength maintenance
Notes high-intent ballistic maximal contractions are most effective style
Explains strong angle specificity (~15-30 degrees transfer range)
Provides a practical example (e.g., pulling into safety pins at a chosen height)
Notes different fatigue profile due to lack of eccentric loading
Cautions that programming prescriptions (duration, frequency, dose) are less standardized
